description: Trigger discrimination for keyword/faceted search design (this skill) vs AI semantic/vector retrieval and its security (rag-security-architect), the base data-store tenancy model (multi-tenant-data-architect), and cursor/pagination mechanics (pagination-cursor-designer).

cases.prompt: Design full-text search over our tenant records: choose the engine, design the indexing pipeline and its freshness, rank results, and guarantee a query for tenant A never returns tenant B's rows.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary) — engine choice, lexical indexing, freshness, ranking, and query+index tenant isolation are keyword-search design. No embeddings/vectors, no base-tenancy redesign, no cursor mechanics are requested.
cases.reason: Lexical/faceted search and its per-tenant isolation are this skill. It extends the data model's tenancy into the index rather than redefining it, and defers vector retrieval and pagination mechanics.

cases.prompt: For our AI assistant, design the vector store and retrieval: embedding model, similarity search, keeping one tenant's embeddings from being retrieved for another, and defending against prompt injection carried in retrieved chunks.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary, reverse) — embeddings, similarity search, vector-store access control, and injection-via-retrieval are AI retrieval security. This is semantic, not lexical, search.
cases.reason: Vector/embedding retrieval and its security (LLM08) are rag-security-architect. search-architecture-designer designs keyword/faceted search; a hybrid defers the vector half there.

cases.prompt: Design our multi-tenant data model: pooled vs siloed storage, how tenant_id scopes every table, and the isolation guarantees at the primary data layer.
cases.reason: The base data-store tenancy model (pooled/siloed, tenant_id scoping of primary tables) is multi-tenant-data-architect. Search consumes that model and extends isolation into the index; it does not define the data-layer tenancy.

cases.prompt: Design our pagination: stable cursors, how we handle deep pagination without offset drift, tie-breaking on non-unique sort keys, and what the cursor encodes.
cases.reason: Cursor encoding, deep-pagination stability, and tie-breaking are pagination-cursor-designer. Search designs the faceted result set and the stable sort/tiebreak, then hands the cursor mechanics over.
